name: Aihualama Trail
tagline: The trail that starts where Manoa Falls ends — climb up into the bamboo forest most people skip.
parking: Use the Manoa Falls parking lot ($7 non-resident, free for residents) at 3739 Manoa Road
bestTime: Morning when the bamboo forest is misty and magical
kamaaina: Free parking with Hawaii state ID
talkStory: About 2.5 miles one-way from the Manoa Falls trailhead with 900 ft elevation gain. The trail branches off just before Manoa Falls and switchbacks steeply up through dense bamboo forest to connect with the Pauoa Flats Trail. It's a completely different vibe from the busy falls trail — quiet, shaded, and you'll probably have it to yourself. The bamboo groves are stunning when the wind blows through them. You can loop it with Pauoa Flats and Nu'uanu Trail for a longer adventure. Muddy and slippery — always.
